taxonomy_v3: Engineering and Development, Machine Learning, Machine Learning Engineer
core_skills: Python, Machine Learning algorithms, Deep Learning frameworks, SQL, Data Visualization
skill_match_scores: Python:1.0, Machine Learning algorithms:1.0, Deep Learning frameworks:1.0, SQL:1.0, Data Visualization:1.0
must_have_quals: Strong proficiency in Python (NumPy, Pandas, Scikit-learn, etc.), Experience with ML algorithms (regression, classification, clustering, NLP, etc.), Hands-on experience with deep learning frameworks (TensorFlow / PyTorch – preferred), Knowledge of SQL and working with large-scale datasets, Experience in data visualization tools (Power BI, Tableau, Matplotlib, Seaborn), Understanding of statistics, probability, and data modeling, Familiarity with cloud platforms (AWS / Azure / GCP) is a plus, Experience with MLOps and model deployment is an advantage
preferred_quals: Bachelor's/Master's degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Statistics, or related field, Experience working in consulting or client-facing roles, Strong problem-solving and communication skills
core_responsibilities: Develop, validate, and deploy machine learning models for real-world business use cases, Perform data cleaning, preprocessing, and feature engineering on large datasets, Conduct exploratory data analysis (EDA) to uncover trends and insights, Collaborate with cross-functional teams including business stakeholders, data engineers, and analysts, Implement and optimize algorithms for performance and scalability, Work on end-to-end model lifecycle: development, testing, deployment, and monitoring, Communicate findings and insights through visualizations and reports, Stay updated with the latest advancements in data science and ML technologies
job_summary: Q1 Technologies, Inc. is seeking a Data Scientist with ML experience to develop, validate, and deploy machine learning models for real-world business use cases. The role involves data cleaning, exploratory data analysis, and collaboration with cross-functional teams.
